stories.headline: US strikes Iranian radar near Hormuz; Iran fires ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain as ceasefire frays
stories.summary: After US forces shot down Iranian drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz, the US struck coastal radar sites at Goruk and on Qeshm Island. Iran's IRGC retaliated with seven ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain late June 5; CENTCOM said six were intercepted and one fell short. Tehran called the strikes a 'flagrant' violation of the April ceasefire, and Gulf states condemned the missile launches.

perspectives.keyFraming: A tit-for-tat exchange over Hormuz access pushes the fragile US-Iran ceasefire to the brink despite ongoing talks.
perspectives.summary: CENTCOM said it downed four Iranian drones headed toward the strait, then struck radar sites at Goruk and Qeshm; Iran responded with seven ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, six of them intercepted. Kuwait and Bahrain condemned the strikes as violations of their sovereignty, and Egypt, Jordan and Qatar issued condemnations. The exchange came amid contradictory signals on ceasefire talks.

sources.title: Iran fires missiles at Gulf after US targets Iranian radar sites
Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire after both sides exchange strikes

perspectives.keyFraming: The US frames its strikes as defensive action to protect maritime traffic and forces after Iranian drone provocations.
perspectives.summary: CENTCOM said it fired on the radar sites to defend against further maritime attacks after Iran launched drones toward the Strait of Hormuz that posed an immediate threat to regional shipping. The US emphasized it intercepted six of seven Iranian ballistic missiles aimed at Gulf allies, casting Iran as the escalating party.

sources.title: US strikes Iran radar sites after drones near Hormuz, missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain

perspectives.keyFraming: Gulf states frame Iran's missiles as 'blatant aggression' violating their sovereignty and endangering their populations.
perspectives.summary: Gulf coverage stressed that Iran's missiles targeting Bahrain and Kuwait drew sharp condemnation from both governments as violations of sovereignty, with further condemnations from Egypt, Jordan and Qatar. The strikes are presented as endangering Gulf populations caught in the US-Iran standoff over Hormuz.

sources.title: Iran Launches New Missile Attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait After US Strikes; Gulf States Condemn 'Blatant Aggression'

stories.headline: Ukraine launches 'unprecedented' deep drone strikes on St Petersburg and the Kronstadt naval base
stories.summary: Ukraine launched extensive overnight drone strikes around St Petersburg, hitting refineries, oil terminals and naval facilities including the Kronstadt base — roughly 1,000 km from Ukraine — on the final day of the SPIEF forum. A Russian official called it 'unprecedented'; the city's governor reported three injured and Kronstadt was closed to traffic. It was the second strike on the Leningrad region in days.

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv's long-range strikes embarrass Putin during his showcase forum and demonstrate deepening reach into Russia.
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drones traveled about 1,000 km to hit naval arsenals and a base at Kronstadt plus oil depots near St Petersburg, with Zelensky highlighting strikes 600 miles from the border. The attack coincided with the close of SPIEF and followed Putin's rejection of Zelensky's offer for direct talks. Russia said hundreds of drones were downed nationwide.

sources.title: Ukraine targets St. Petersburg in what Russia calls an 'unprecedented' attack
Ukraine launches large-scale drone attack as Putin meets Schroder

perspectives.keyFraming: Russian officials frame the assault as a mass attack repelled by air defenses, stressing civilian injuries and downplaying damage.
perspectives.summary: Via Russian officials and TASS, St Petersburg governor Aleksandr Beglov said the city came under a large-scale drone attack with three people injured, and that Kronstadt was closed to traffic for several hours. Russia said hundreds of Ukrainian drones were downed as the SPIEF forum concluded, emphasizing the scale of the defensive interception.

sources.title: Russia says hundreds of Ukrainian drones downed as economic forum ends

stories.headline: Federal judge strikes down Trump policy that froze asylum and immigration benefits for 39 countries
stories.summary: On June 5, US District Chief Judge John McConnell Jr. issued a 135-page ruling vacating Trump administration policies that categorically barred nationals of 39 countries from final decisions on asylum, work permits, green cards and citizenship. He found the freeze 'arbitrary and capricious' and ordered processing to resume; the administration is expected to appeal to the First Circuit.

perspectives.keyFraming: A scathing rebuke finding Trump put hundreds of thousands of immigrants' lives 'on hold' based solely on country of birth.
perspectives.summary: CNN reports McConnell struck down restrictions on processing asylum, work-permit, green-card and citizenship applications for 39 African, Asian, Latin American and Middle Eastern nations, calling the policies unlawful and arbitrary. The 135-page opinion said the moves placed the lives of countless individuals on hold.

sources.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump admin limits on asylum and immigration applications

perspectives.keyFraming: The judge says the post-shooting freeze threw immigrants into 'indeterminate legal limbo,' reopening decisions for hundreds of thousands.
perspectives.summary: PBS NewsHour reports the Rhode Island-based judge ruled USCIS ignored the law in halting decisions for immigrants from 39 countries after a National Guard shooting. The ruling reopens pending asylum, work-permit, green-card and citizenship applications; the government can appeal to the First Circuit.

sources.title: Judge strikes down Trump policy that halted asylum decisions for 39 countries
US Judge Strikes Down Trump Policies Targeting Immigrants From 39 Countries

perspectives.keyFraming: A judge blocks a national-security-driven immigration freeze enacted after an immigrant shot National Guard members.
perspectives.summary: Coverage notes the policy affecting 39 countries followed the shooting of National Guard members and that the Trump administration is expected to appeal. The framing emphasizes the security rationale behind pausing benefits for nationals from countries tied to the travel ban.

sources.title: A federal judge strikes down Trump administration immigration policy affecting 39 countries

stories.headline: Strong May jobs report (172k) sinks rate-cut hopes as Warsh's first FOMC nears; hike bets build
stories.summary: The US added 172,000 jobs in May, roughly double expectations, with upward revisions to prior months; unemployment held at 4.3%. The print swept away near-term rate-cut hopes and shifted the debate toward possible hikes ahead of new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting (June 16-17). 10-year yields jumped toward 4.54% and the 30-year topped 5%.

perspectives.keyFraming: A hot labor market plus sticky inflation boxes in the new chair as 'good news is bad news' for rate cuts.
perspectives.summary: Coverage stresses that resilient hiring removes the Fed's rationale for easing and leaves Warsh managing colleagues who increasingly favor hikes to contain persistent inflation, complicating Trump's demand for lower rates at the chair's debut meeting.

sources.title: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts further out of reach as Chair Warsh faces policy tests

perspectives.keyFraming: Solid jobs underpin Warsh's debut but inflation squeezes households, leaving the rate path genuinely uncertain.
perspectives.summary: Wire and market coverage frames the report as partly validating a steady-hand approach while noting inflation continues to pressure consumers, leaving the rate path uncertain into the June meeting.

sources.title: US Jobs Report for May Will Partly Underpin Warsh's Fed Debut

perspectives.keyFraming: A strong economy gives Warsh room to tighten as rate-hike support grows among Fed officials.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning framing emphasizes labor-market strength as evidence the economy can absorb tighter policy, highlighting growing internal support for hikes that Warsh must weigh against White House pressure.

sources.title: Kevin Warsh faces support for rate hikes amid strong jobs report
